item.title: Site Updates 2026 — Part 3
item.description:
I’d been making various updates but forgot to write them down…

Here are the user-facing feature updates (summarized by Opus 4.6):

  1. Internationalization (i18n)
  • Added 3-language support: Japanese (ja), English (en), and Traditional Chinese (tw)
  • Full English and Traditional Chinese translations across the entire site
  1. Major Projects Page Overhaul
  • Simplified card display: removed summary and tech tags from cards, showing only title + date
  • Category / Timeline toggle: two view modes (by category / by year timeline)
  • Added date display to modals
  1. Support Feature Revamp
  • Migrated from OFUSE to GitHub Sponsors
  1. Homepage Redesign
  • Author avatar display: created home_info.html to show avatar image on the homepage

item.title: MyShorui
item.description:
Overview

A tool for generating invoice PDFs that comply with Japanese business customs.
Supports the Qualified Invoice System (適格請求書等保存方式).

item.title: Codex Meetup Tokyo #1
item.description:
Overview

Gave a lightning talk at Codex Meetup Tokyo #1, held on March 19, 2026.
The talk was titled “Codex’s Strengths Through Spec-Driven Development,” highlighting three strengths of Codex in spec-driven development (SDD): the GPT model’s diligence, stable Agent Skills invocation, and rigorous review capabilities.

Codex's Strengths Through Spec-Driven Development
https://speakerdeck.com/beagle_dog_inu/shi-yang-qu-dong-kai-fa-dejian-rucodexnoqiang-mi
Codex Meetup Tokyo #1
https://aiau.connpass.com/event/384679/

item.title: MyKaikei
item.description:
Overview

A tool for managing and generating the ledgers and financial statements required for blue-form tax filing as a sole proprietor.
When you describe a transaction, it determines the account, sub-account, and consumption tax category, then appends the entry to the journal.
Journal entries (creating bookkeeping records) can also be delegated to Claude or other coding agents, enabling semi-automated daily bookkeeping.
It also generates financial statements from the journal to support year-end tax return preparation.

item.title: Vibe Coding Feature from Software Design Feb Issue
item.description:
Overview

Appeared as a panelist at an offline talk session bringing together all five contributors of the lead feature “Complete Guide to Vibe Coding” from Software Design magazine’s February 2026 issue.
The session was conducted in a free-form format where panelists discussed given topics.

Vibe Coding Feature from Software Design Feb Issue
https://wnb.connpass.com/event/383711/

item.title: cxreset
item.description:
Overview

A statusline-oriented script that shows usage for Codex’s 5h/7day quotas and the time remaining until reset.
Designed to be used as an add-on for ccstatusline.

cxreset - npm
https://www.npmjs.com/package/cxreset
cxreset - GitHub
https://github.com/beagleworks/cxreset

item.title: ccclog
item.description:
Overview

A static web app for browsing Claude Code and Codex change histories in side-by-side Japanese and English tables.
Supports automatic fetching, update dates, monthly update counts, and content search.

Claude Code CHANGELOG Viewer
https://beagleworks.github.io/ccclog
ccclog - GitHub
https://github.com/beagleworks/ccclog

item.title: Preposition Quiz
item.description:
Overview

A learning app where you choose the correct preposition from four options.
If you get it wrong, you get a soft, AI-generated quip in response.
Built in 1.5 hours during the event “AI Meetup / Let’s Build Something Together with Claude Max Plan.”

Preposition Quiz
https://beagleworks.github.io/preposition-quiz/
Preposition Quiz - GitHub
https://github.com/beagleworks/preposition-quiz

item.title: Software Design February 2026
item.description:
Overview

Contributed to Chapter 1, “Getting Started with Vibe Coding,” in Feature 1: Vibe Coding Complete Guide.
It explains the basics of Vibe Coding, how to approach it, and what it can change in practice through concrete examples in accessible language.

Software Design February 2026 Issue
https://gihyo.jp/magazine/SD/archive/2026/202602
Software Design February 2026 Issue | Kindle Store | Amazon
https://amzn.to/3NBD7V7

item.title: ccreset
item.description:
Overview

A statusline-oriented script that shows usage for Claude Code’s 5h/7day quotas and the time remaining until reset.
Designed to be used as an add-on for ccstatusline.
Development has ended as Claude Code has implemented equivalent functionality natively.

ccreset - npm
https://www.npmjs.com/package/ccreset
ccreset - GitHub
https://github.com/beagleworks/ccreset

item.title: How Boris Explained Using Claude Code in X Replies
item.description:
Overview

An article collecting especially useful points from Boris Cherny’s replies on X about how to use Claude Code.
Received more than 50 Likes.

How Boris Explained Using Claude Code in X Replies
https://zenn.dev/beagle/articles/_0019_keypoints_from_responses_of_boris

item.title: 2026 Site Update #2
item.description:
Today I noticed a lot of talk about a potential return to personal websites.
Personal sites are fun, and when you can use Claude Code for customization, it makes you want to keep adding more.

What I Did

  • Changed the Archive page display
  • Changed article header/footer display and added OFUSE

item.title: New Year Site Maintenance
item.description:
I sent my greetings on X, but happy new year — looking forward to another great year.

What I Did

  • Preserved uppercase tag names on the Tags page (overrode theme in layouts/_default/terms.html, changed .Name to .LinkTitle)
  • Moved tag display from the bottom of articles to below the date (in the header) (layouts/_default/single.html)

Future Plans

As always…

  • Gradually update the Projects page content
  • Write articles

item.title: On Releasing Setsuna, a Cross-Device Text Sharing Service
item.description:
As the title says, I released Setsuna, a cross-device text sharing service, on 12/28.

(The sample demo has been taken down.)

デバイス間テキスト共有アプリSetsunaをリリースしました。https://t.co/FNbVd8ohHOhttps://t.co/8VPNjyX0K7

昔自分用につくって公開していたOSSを、Claude Codeに全面バックアップしてもらってリバイバルしたものです。…

— びーぐる (@beagle_dog_inu) December 28, 2025

(Since it was abbreviated, I’ll write it out here)
“This is a revival of an OSS I made for personal use a while back, with full backing from Claude Code. There are plenty of similar tools out there now and it’s nothing special, but I wanted to rebuild it once with modern tech as a learning exercise, so I released it.”

item.title: Reading 'Introduction to AI-Driven Development with Claude Code'
item.description:
I read “Introduction to AI-Driven Development with Claude Code” (Claude Code によるAI駆動開発入門).

I shared some of my thoughts on X.

池袋に行ったのでジュンク堂でClaude CodeによるAI駆動開発入門、買いました。これはもういい意味で教科書ですね。使ったことないひとは体系的に学べる、使ったことあるひとはパラパラめくるだけで何かしら発見があるようなイメージ。 pic.twitter.com/fUPNrjwPQz

— びーぐる (@beagle_dog_inu) December 10, 2025

既にほとんど知っている事柄に関して、他の人の異なるアプローチや考え方にふれる、記事や書籍を読む、ってのは本当によいことで。
新たな知識は増えないけど、代わりに新たなインスピレーションや気付きは発生するのでとても大事にしている。当然理解も深まり言語化もうまくなる。

— びーぐる (@beagle_dog_inu) December 20, 2025

I also spoke about how great this book is to several people at the Claude Code Meetup Tokyo I recently attended.

Claude Code Meetup Tokyo · Luma
https://luma.com/ioed4ksj?tk=U0Wvfo

Beyond covering the basics of Claude Code in a textbook-like manner, what makes this book really stand out is that you can see what the author, Hirakawa-san, instructs Claude Code to do for a given requirement, and understand “what they’re thinking.” Even if you already know Claude Code well, reading from this perspective will teach you a lot. I certainly learned a great deal.

item.title: AI Tool Usage and Impressions: End-of-2025 Edition
item.description:
Overview

An article summarizing the AI-related subscriptions I was using at the end of 2025 and my impressions of them.
Earned one Zenn badge.

AI Tool Usage and Impressions: End-of-2025 Edition
https://zenn.dev/beagle/articles/_0018_2025_12_my_subscriptions_to_ai_tools

item.title: Setsuna
item.description:
Overview

A site for sharing text across different devices.
Rooms created on the service are automatically deleted after 24 hours.
This is a revival project of an OSS app I had built in the past.

Setsuna - GitHub
https://github.com/beagleworks/Setsuna

item.title: Claude Code Meetup Tokyo #aimeetup
item.description:
Overview

Knowledge-sharing slides prepared for Claude Code Meetup Tokyo (#aimeetup) held on December 22, 2025.
The talk was titled “How to Keep Claude Code Honest in Tests.”
Proposes ideas for using Rules and Skills to prevent Claude Code from writing sloppy tests.

How to Keep Claude Code Honest in Tests
https://speakerdeck.com/beagle_dog_inu/claude-codenitesutodele-wosasenaiji-shu
Claude Code Meetup Tokyo #aimeetup
https://aimeetup.connpass.com/event/378288/

item.title: iCUE Restarter
item.description:
Overview

A Windows system tray app built in C# to solve an issue where Corsair iCUE fails to recognize the keyboard after waking from sleep.

iCUE Restarter
https://github.com/beagleworks/iCUERestarter

item.title: Day 6
item.description:
Wasn’t planning to have a Day 6, but here we are…

What I Did

  • Implemented category grouping for the Projects page

Future Plans

  • Gradually update the Projects page content
  • Write articles

item.title: Day 5
item.description:
Finished the remaining tasks — now it’s just about filling in the content.

What I Did

  • Checked mobile responsiveness
  • Checked share button behavior
  • Created OGP images

Future Plans

  • Write Projects page content

item.title: Day 4
item.description:
I think this can be considered done for now.

What I Did

  • Implemented link cards
  • Created the Projects page
    • Templates and such
    • Modal windows

Future Plans

  • Adjust Projects page styling
  • Write Projects page content
  • Check mobile responsiveness
  • Check share button behavior

item.title: Day 3
item.description:
Been busy so there was a bit of a gap.
Proper work starts tomorrow.

What I Did

  • Fleshed out the About page
  • Planned the Projects page

Future Plans

  • Check share button behavior
  • Create Projects page

item.title: Day 2
item.description:
Did some renovations between tasks.

Design Changes

  • Changed fonts
  • Adjusted line spacing
  • Changed heading design

Content & Feature Additions

  • Added About page
  • Improved search functionality

Future Plans

  • Check share button behavior
  • Create Projects page

item.title: The Site
item.description:
The Site…?

Yes.
I write technical articles on Zenn, so this is a place for everything that doesn’t belong there.

QUIET QUALIA

That’s the name of the site.
There’s probably no deep meaning behind it. Maybe.

How Was It Built?

Hugo + PaperMod
Hugo is great because it’s easy to customize. And if you get stuck, you can just ask an AI.

Main Customizations (Notes)

  • Hatena Bookmark share button
  • Zenn-style author block (below articles)
  • Author icon

I think that’s about it…?

item.title: Quiet Qualia
item.description:
Overview

The website of Beagle Works.
Features a blog, portfolio, and more.
